subject_tmpl: Water bill jumped to {AMOUNT} — account {ACCT}
body_tmpl: Hi, I'm {FIRST} {LAST} at {ADDRESS}. My water bill this cycle came in at {AMOUNT}, which is nearly triple what I normally pay. I haven't changed anything — same household, same habits. Account number is {ACCT}. Can someone please look into whether there was a misread or an error on my meter? I'd appreciate a call at {PHONE} if possible.
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST}, thank you for reaching out. We've pulled account {ACCT} and flagged it for a meter re-read, which should be completed within {DAYS} business days. If the re-read confirms an error, we will issue an adjusted bill automatically. You'll receive a notice by email or mail once it's resolved. Feel free to call us with any questions.

subject_tmpl: Possible meter misread — {STREET} account
body_tmpl: Good morning. I believe my meter was misread last month. The bill for {ADDRESS} shows {AMOUNT} but my average is well under half that. I checked the meter myself and the reading doesn't match what's on the bill. Account {ACCT}. Please advise on next steps — do I need to submit a photo?

subject_tmpl: Estimated reads on my account for {DAYS} straight months
body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST} here, {ADDRESS}. I've noticed my last {DAYS} bills all say 'estimated read.' Nobody has come to actually read the meter. This month the estimate jumped to {AMOUNT} and I'm not comfortable paying that without a real reading. Can you schedule an actual meter read or tell me how to self-report? Account {ACCT}.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST} — you're right to flag this. We'll schedule a field read at {ADDRESS} within the next {DAYS} business days. In the meantime, if you'd like to submit your own meter reading, you can call {PHONE} or reply here with a photo and the reading digits. We appreciate your patience.

subject_tmpl: Autopay did not process — shutoff warning received
body_tmpl: I received a shutoff warning dated {DATE} but I'm enrolled in autopay and the payment should have come out automatically. My bank shows no debit from the Village. Account {ACCT}, address {ADDRESS}. I don't want to lose service over a system glitch. What happened and how do I get the shutoff notice cancelled?
reply_tmpl: Thank you for contacting us. We see account {ACCT} shows autopay enrolled but the {DATE} payment did not process — likely due to a card update or bank change. Please call {PHONE} to provide updated payment info and we will place a 10-day hold on the shutoff while you do so. No service interruption will occur if payment is received within that window.

subject_tmpl: Request for payment plan — past-due balance {AMOUNT}
body_tmpl: Hello, I've fallen behind on my water bill and the balance is now {AMOUNT} on account {ACCT}. I want to pay but cannot do it all at once. I'm a senior on a fixed income at {ADDRESS}. Is there a payment arrangement I can apply for? I've been a Bellwood resident for over {AGE} years and have never had this problem before.
reply_tmpl: Hello, we understand these situations and we're happy to help. Bellwood offers payment plans for past-due balances. Please call our office at {PHONE} so we can review account {ACCT} and set up an arrangement that fits your budget. We also have a senior discount program — ask about it when you call.

subject_tmpl: Senior discount — do I qualify at {ADDRESS}?
body_tmpl: I am {AGE} years old and own my home at {ADDRESS}. A neighbor mentioned the Village has a senior water discount. I'd like to know if I qualify and how to apply. My account number should be {ACCT}. I'm on a fixed income and every bit helps. Please let me know what documentation you need.

subject_tmpl: Shutoff notice — I already paid {AMOUNT} on {DATE}
body_tmpl: I'm writing because I got a shutoff notice in the mail today but I paid {AMOUNT} online on {DATE}. Account {ACCT} at {ADDRESS}. I have the confirmation number. Did the payment not go through? Please check before sending anyone to shut off service — this would be a real hardship.
reply_tmpl: We located your payment of {AMOUNT} on account {ACCT} — it posted and the shutoff order has been cancelled. We apologize for the confusion; the notice was generated before the payment cleared our system. No further action is needed on your part.

subject_tmpl: High usage alert — checking for leak on {STREET}
body_tmpl: We got a high-usage alert on account {ACCT} for {ADDRESS}. The bill says {AMOUNT}. We've checked inside and don't see any obvious leaks. Could this be a running toilet we haven't caught? Or could it be a meter issue? We want to resolve it before next cycle. Can someone advise or send a tech to inspect the meter?

subject_tmpl: Bill for vacant house — property at {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST} here. I own {ADDRESS} but the property has been vacant since {DATE}. I'm still receiving water bills — the most recent is for {AMOUNT} on account {ACCT}. There should be zero usage at a vacant property. Is there a leak at the main, or can the meter be shut off until I find a tenant?
reply_tmpl: Thank you for letting us know. We can schedule a meter inspection at {ADDRESS} to check for a line leak. If the property is vacant and you'd like service suspended, please submit a written request and we can temporarily deactivate the account. Call {PHONE} to coordinate or reply here with your preferred date.

subject_tmpl: New owner at {ADDRESS} — bill in prior owner's name
body_tmpl: We closed on {ADDRESS} on {DATE} and I need to transfer the water account to my name. It's currently in the prior owner's name. I don't want to inherit any past-due balance or have service interrupted. Account {ACCT}. Can you let me know what forms I need to submit and where to send them?

subject_tmpl: Double charge on account {ACCT} this cycle
body_tmpl: Hi, I'm {FIRST} at {ADDRESS}. Looking at my latest statement I see two charges dated {DATE} totaling {AMOUNT} — it looks like I was billed twice for the same period. Can you review account {ACCT} and refund or credit the duplicate? I'd rather not call if this can be resolved by email.
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST}, we've reviewed account {ACCT} and confirmed a duplicate charge occurred on {DATE}. A credit of {AMOUNT} has been applied to your account and will appear on your next statement. We apologize for the inconvenience.

subject_tmpl: Never received bill — now I get a shutoff notice?
body_tmpl: I have not received a water bill at {ADDRESS} in {DAYS} days, and today I got a shutoff notice for {AMOUNT} past due. I did not know I owed anything. My account is {ACCT}. Is my address correct in your system? I may have a mail delivery issue. I'd like to switch to email billing going forward.

subject_tmpl: Sewer charge question on bill — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: My bill for account {ACCT} at {ADDRESS} shows a sewer charge of {AMOUNT} separately from the water charge. I wasn't aware I was being billed for sewer. Is this standard? Has this always been on my bill and I missed it, or is this a new fee? The total jumped significantly from last cycle.
reply_tmpl: The sewer charge on your bill reflects the Village's cost to treat and convey wastewater. It has been part of water bills for several years and is calculated based on your water usage. The increase you're seeing is likely tied to higher water consumption this cycle. If you believe there's an error, please call {PHONE} and we'll review account {ACCT} in detail.

subject_tmpl: Winter freeze — burst pipe and sky-high bill
body_tmpl: During the cold snap around {DATE} a pipe burst inside our house at {ADDRESS}. We didn't catch it for a couple of days. Now the bill is {AMOUNT} for account {ACCT}. We've already repaired the pipe. Is there any kind of leak adjustment credit the Village offers for situations like this? We've never had an issue before.
reply_tmpl: We're sorry to hear about the pipe. Bellwood does offer a one-time leak adjustment for documented repairs. Please submit a plumber's receipt or proof of repair to our office and we'll review account {ACCT} for a credit on the excess usage. The adjustment generally covers up to 50% of the overage above your 12-month average.

subject_tmpl: Summer irrigation — is there a separate meter option?
body_tmpl: I'm {FIRST} {LAST} at {ADDRESS}. I water my lawn and garden heavily in summer and my water bills spike because I'm also paying sewer rates on that outdoor water. A friend in another suburb told me they have a separate irrigation meter that isn't subject to sewer charges. Does Bellwood offer anything like that? Account {ACCT}.

subject_tmpl: Payment plan extension request — account {ACCT}
body_tmpl: I'm currently on a payment plan for account {ACCT} at {ADDRESS}. My plan payment of {AMOUNT} was due {DATE} but I had unexpected medical expenses this month. I'm not asking to skip it — I just need {DAYS} more days. Can someone extend the due date so my service isn't interrupted? I'll pay in full by the extended date.
reply_tmpl: Hello, we've noted your request for a {DAYS}-day extension on account {ACCT}. This has been approved and your next plan payment due date is updated accordingly. Please ensure payment is received by the new date to keep the plan active. Thank you for communicating with us proactively.

subject_tmpl: Cannot access online portal — payment due soon
body_tmpl: I've been trying to log into the water payment portal for {DAYS} days and keep getting an error. My bill for {AMOUNT} is due {DATE} and I don't want a late fee. Account {ACCT}, property at {ADDRESS}. Can you reset my account access or take a payment by phone? I'll call {PHONE} if that's easier.

subject_tmpl: Rate increase question — bill up {AMOUNT} from last year
body_tmpl: I'm comparing my current bill to the same period last year and it's up {AMOUNT} even though my usage hasn't changed. Did Bellwood raise water rates? I didn't receive any notice. I'm at {ADDRESS}, account {ACCT}. If there was a rate change, can you point me to where it was published so I can understand the breakdown?
reply_tmpl: The Village Board approved a rate adjustment effective {DATE} to fund capital infrastructure improvements. Notices were mailed to all accounts and posted on the Village website. Your current bill on account {ACCT} reflects the new rates. The rate schedule is also available at Village Hall. We apologize if the mailed notice didn't reach you.

subject_tmpl: Final bill after move-out — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: I moved out of {ADDRESS} on {DATE}. Can you generate a final water bill for account {ACCT} and close the account? My forwarding address is on {STREET} in Bellwood. I want to make sure there's no balance left that could go to collections. Please send the final bill to the new address.

subject_tmpl: Low water pressure AND high bill — something's wrong
body_tmpl: We've had low water pressure at {ADDRESS} for about {DAYS} days, and at the same time our bill came in at {AMOUNT} — way above normal for account {ACCT}. If our pressure is low, how can usage be so high? It makes me think there might be a main leak somewhere on our street near {CROSS}. Can someone come look?
reply_tmpl: That combination — low pressure paired with elevated usage — can indicate a leak between the main and your meter. We'll dispatch a crew to inspect the area near {CROSS} and check your meter at {ADDRESS}. In the meantime, please check your property for any wet spots or soggy ground near the meter box.

subject_tmpl: Disputing {AMOUNT} charge — meter not accessible
body_tmpl: I want to dispute the {AMOUNT} charge on my bill for {DATE}. The meter is in our basement and I know for a fact nobody knocked or left a notice to access it. So how was a non-estimated read taken? Account {ACCT}, {ADDRESS}. If this was estimated, it should say so on the bill. I'd like a written explanation.

subject_tmpl: Request hardship waiver on late fee — account {ACCT}
body_tmpl: I paid my water bill late this cycle because I was in the hospital for {DAYS} days following an emergency. A late fee of {AMOUNT} was added to account {ACCT} for {ADDRESS}. I have a hospital discharge summary to document the situation. Is there a process to request a one-time fee waiver given the circumstances?
reply_tmpl: We're sorry to hear about your health situation. Please email or bring in documentation of the hospitalization and we will submit a fee waiver request for account {ACCT} to the Finance Director for approval. We process these on a case-by-case basis and will notify you within {DAYS} business days.

subject_tmpl: Autopay enrollment — how do I sign up?
body_tmpl: Hello, I keep forgetting to pay my water bill and want to enroll in autopay so it drafts automatically. My account is {ACCT} at {ADDRESS}. Is there an online form or do I need to come to Village Hall? I'd prefer bank draft over credit card if that's an option. Thanks.
reply_tmpl: You can enroll in autopay through the online customer portal or by completing a form at Village Hall, 3200 Washington Blvd. Bank draft (ACH) is available at no additional fee. Once enrolled, payments for account {ACCT} will draft on the due date each cycle. Let us know if you need the portal link.

subject_tmpl: Bill went to landlord not tenant — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: I rent at {ADDRESS} and my lease says I'm responsible for water. But the bill keeps going to my landlord. He gave me the account number {ACCT} but the mailing address is wrong. The current balance is {AMOUNT} and I need to pay it. Can you update the mailing info to my name at this address so I get the bills directly?

subject_tmpl: Water discoloration AND billing concern — {STREET}
body_tmpl: Two issues I need help with at {ADDRESS}: First, our water has been slightly brownish since {DATE} — I assume it's from work on {STREET}. Second, the bill for {AMOUNT} this cycle seems off given that we cut back usage during the discoloration. Account {ACCT}. Can you address both or route the water quality part to the right department?
reply_tmpl: Thank you for flagging both issues. We've forwarded the water quality concern to Public Works for follow-up — discoloration near active work zones is typically temporary and not a health risk. On the billing side, we'll hold off on any late penalties on account {ACCT} until we can confirm the read was accurate. Expect contact within {DAYS} days.

subject_tmpl: Wrong address on bill — account {ACCT}
body_tmpl: My water bill for account {ACCT} shows {ADDRESS} but I moved to {STREET} three months ago. The service address is correct but the mailing address is wrong and I've been missing my bills. Balance is now {AMOUNT}. Can you update the mailing address going forward and waive any late fees caused by the wrong address?

subject_tmpl: Landlord dispute — water shutoff threat at {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: I'm a tenant at {ADDRESS}. My landlord is supposed to pay the water bill but I received a shutoff notice for {AMOUNT} due {DATE} on account {ACCT}. I am not the account holder. I cannot be left without water. What are my options as a tenant? Is there any way the Village can notify tenants separately about shutoffs?
reply_tmpl: We understand how distressing this is. Illinois law provides certain tenant protections regarding utility shutoffs. While the account is in the landlord's name, we can accept payment from any party to prevent shutoff. You may also want to contact Cook County's tenant services line. Please call our office at {PHONE} so we can walk you through your options on account {ACCT}.

subject_tmpl: Bill timing — why is my cycle shorter this month?
body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}, account {ACCT}. My bill this cycle covers only {DAYS} days instead of the usual 30 and it's still {AMOUNT}. That's a higher per-day rate than normal. Was the billing cycle changed? Or is there a fee I'm not understanding? The bill isn't very clear on the breakdown.

subject_tmpl: Check payment mailed {DAYS} days ago — not posted
body_tmpl: I mailed a check for {AMOUNT} on {DATE} to pay account {ACCT} at {ADDRESS}. As of today it still hasn't posted and I'm worried it got lost. I don't want to stop payment without knowing if it's in transit. Can you check if it arrived? If it hasn't I'll issue a replacement payment right away.
reply_tmpl: We checked account {ACCT} and no check has posted as of today. Given the time elapsed, we'd recommend stopping payment on the original check and submitting a new payment online or in person to avoid a shutoff. No late fee will be assessed given the circumstances. Please let us know once you've resubmitted.

subject_tmpl: Senior discount application — {FIRST} {LAST}
body_tmpl: I'm {AGE} years old and have lived at {ADDRESS} my entire adult life. I recently learned that Bellwood offers a senior discount on water bills. I'm enclosing a copy of my ID. My water account is {ACCT}. Please send me the application form or let me know if I can complete it by email. Thank you.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for reaching out. You can pick up the Senior Utility Discount application at Village Hall, 3200 Washington Blvd, or we can mail one to {ADDRESS}. You'll need a copy of your ID and proof of age. Once approved, the discount will be applied starting with the next billing cycle for account {ACCT}.

subject_tmpl: Meter reading vs. my own reading — big discrepancy
body_tmpl: I took a photo of my meter on {DATE} and it reads {NUM}. But the bill for account {ACCT} at {ADDRESS} shows a reading of {NUM} as the current read, which is higher than what I photographed. How is that possible? The bill charges me for {AMOUNT}. I'd like to submit the photo as evidence of the correct reading.

subject_tmpl: Identity mix-up — someone else's account on my bill
body_tmpl: I got a water bill addressed to a different name at {ADDRESS}. My name is {FIRST} {LAST} and I've owned this property since {DATE}. Account number on the bill is {ACCT} which isn't one I recognize. I'm concerned about an identity or data entry mix-up. Can someone sort this out quickly?
reply_tmpl: Thank you for flagging this. There appears to have been a data entry issue with account {ACCT}. We've escalated it to our billing supervisor and will have it corrected within {DAYS} business days. You will receive a corrected bill under your name. Please don't make any payment on the incorrectly addressed bill in the meantime.

subject_tmpl: Irrigation leak credit — submitted repair receipts
body_tmpl: Following up on my earlier email about the underground irrigation leak at {ADDRESS}. I've attached the plumber's invoice and repair receipt showing work completed on {DATE}. The excess water usage came to roughly {AMOUNT} above my normal bill. Account {ACCT}. Has the credit application been reviewed yet? Let me know if you need anything else.

subject_tmpl: New construction — when does billing start?
body_tmpl: I'm building a new home at {ADDRESS} and recently got a temporary water connection through the permit office. Can you tell me when billing begins on a new construction account and how the meter gets registered? I want to make sure I don't have a balance building up that I don't know about. Contact me at {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Billing on new construction accounts begins once the permanent meter is set and the account is activated. Temporary construction water service is billed separately through the permit. Once your Certificate of Occupancy is issued, contact our office to set up the permanent account so there's no gap or accidental balance. Call us at {PHONE} any time.

subject_tmpl: Commercial account {ACCT} — spike to {AMOUNT} unexplained
body_tmpl: This is {NAME} at {BIZ}, {ADDRESS}. Our water bill for the last billing period hit {AMOUNT}, which is more than double our typical monthly charge. We have not changed operations or added equipment. Account {ACCT}. Before I dispute this formally, can your office pull the read history and let me know if this looks like a meter error or actual consumption?
reply_tmpl: Hello, we've pulled the read history for account {ACCT} and are comparing it against your 12-month average. We'll have an analysis ready within {DAYS} business days. If the data points to a meter error, we'll issue a corrected bill. If it suggests actual high usage, we can discuss next steps including an on-site inspection.

subject_tmpl: Payment plan for {BIZ} — past due {AMOUNT}
body_tmpl: {NAME}, owner of {BIZ} at {ADDRESS}. We've had a rough few months and our water account {ACCT} is past due {AMOUNT}. I want to stay current and keep service on. Can the Village work out a structured payment plan? I can pay {AMOUNT} monthly starting {DATE}. Happy to come into Village Hall to sign any agreement.

subject_tmpl: Grease trap inspection triggering extra sewer charge?
body_tmpl: We operate {BIZ} at {ADDRESS} and noticed a new 'supplemental sewer' charge of {AMOUNT} on account {ACCT} starting this cycle. The only change we made was a required grease trap upgrade in {DATE}. Is the new charge related to the upgrade, or is this a billing error? Please advise before our next payment.
reply_tmpl: The supplemental sewer charge reflects updated flow calculations tied to your upgraded grease trap, which is now rated for higher throughput. This is consistent with Village ordinance on commercial sewer billing. If you believe the flow rating is incorrect, you may request a review — please call {PHONE} to start that process on account {ACCT}.

subject_tmpl: Approval needed: fee waiver request — account {ACCT}
body_tmpl: David, I have a fee waiver request from a resident at {ADDRESS} for a {AMOUNT} late fee on account {ACCT}. The resident was hospitalized for {DAYS} days and has documentation. Per our policy this requires Finance Director sign-off. Supporting docs attached. Please advise by {DATE} so I can notify the resident before their next due date.
reply_tmpl: Lorena, approved. Please apply the credit and document it as 'hardship waiver' in the billing notes. Let me know if we see a pattern on this account going forward.

subject_tmpl: Field read request — accounts flagged for estimated reads {STREET}
body_tmpl: Frank, I'm sending over a list of {NUM} accounts on {STREET} and {CROSS} that have been on estimated reads for {DAYS}+ days. Residents are calling in to dispute bills. Can you schedule crew to do physical reads this week? I'll hold disputes until we have actuals. The accounts are attached.

subject_tmpl: Quarterly delinquency report due {DATE}
body_tmpl: Lorena, please pull the quarterly delinquency list — all accounts with balances over {AMOUNT} past due more than {DAYS} days. I need it by {DATE} for the Board packet. Include account numbers, service addresses, balance owed, and last payment date. We'll use this to initiate the next shutoff cycle.
reply_tmpl: David, report is ready and attached. There are {NUM} accounts meeting the criteria. I've flagged {NUM} that have active payment plans — recommend we exclude those from the shutoff list. Let me know if you need it formatted differently for the Board.

subject_tmpl: Resident complaint escalation — account {ACCT}, {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Karen, flagging for awareness: the resident at {ADDRESS} (account {ACCT}) has now emailed three times disputing a bill for {AMOUNT}. We've done a re-read that confirmed the original amount. Resident is now demanding to speak with the Mayor. Recommend we schedule a call with the resident and Finance by {DATE} to resolve before it escalates further.

subject_tmpl: Main break on {STREET} — billing hold for affected accounts
body_tmpl: Lorena, we had a main break on {STREET} near {CROSS} repaired on {DATE}. Flow data during the break will inflate usage readings for nearby meters. I'd estimate {NUM} accounts are affected. Please flag those accounts for billing review — we shouldn't be charging residents for water that was lost to the break. I'll send you the affected address list by end of day.
reply_tmpl: Tom, thanks for the heads-up. I'll place a billing hold on the flagged accounts once I get your list. I'll also note the reason in each account file so we have documentation if residents call in. Let me know the estimated credit amount once you have it from the loss calculation.

subject_tmpl: Mayor — water bills are out of control on {STREET}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I'm {FIRST} {LAST} and I've lived on {STREET} for {AGE} years. Several neighbors and I have all seen our water bills double in recent cycles. We're not wasting water — this is a systemic problem. My bill this month is {AMOUNT} on account {ACCT}. I'm asking you personally to look into what's happening on this block.
reply_tmpl: Dear {FIRST}, thank you for writing directly. I take billing concerns seriously and have asked our Water Billing office to review the accounts flagged by residents on {STREET}. You should receive a follow-up from Lorena Diaz within {DAYS} business days. I appreciate you bringing this to my attention.

subject_tmpl: Elderly neighbor facing shutoff — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I'm writing on behalf of my elderly neighbor at {ADDRESS}. She is {AGE} years old, lives alone, and received a shutoff notice for {AMOUNT} on account {ACCT}. She doesn't know how to navigate the billing office and is frightened. She's a longtime Bellwood resident. Is there anything your office can do to help her?
reply_tmpl: Thank you for being a good neighbor and reaching out. My assistant Tina will contact Lorena Diaz in Water Billing to ensure a senior outreach call is placed to {ADDRESS} today. We will explore all available assistance options including the senior discount and payment plan before any shutoff proceeds.

subject_tmpl: Thank you for resolving my billing issue — account {ACCT}
body_tmpl: Hi, I just wanted to say thank you to whoever handled my water billing dispute for account {ACCT} at {ADDRESS}. The corrected bill showed up in the mail and it's right. I know these things can take time and I appreciate how quickly your office turned it around. Bellwood is lucky to have staff that actually respond.
